title: fix(terminal): make standard terminals robust against xterm DOM renderer pausing
summary: xterm.js 6.0 DOM renderer IntersectionObserver can silently set _isPaused=true; needs layered recovery (onWriteParsed, heartbeat, focus/visibility events). Also: terminal.open(host) is mandatory when creating fresh instances for hibernation restore, guarded by a dimension check.

title: feat(theme): circular reveal animation for theme switches
summary: document.startViewTransition(cb) runs cb asynchronously after current paint — Zustand/React values captured in the closure are stale. Always call store.getState() inside the callback. Same applies to rAF/setTimeout/idle callbacks that apply store state.

title: fix(e2e): propagate SKIP_FIRST_RUN_DIALOGS flag to sandboxed renderer
summary: Vite bakes import.meta.env at build time — env vars set only at Electron launch (E2E flags, runtime config) are always undefined in the sandboxed renderer. Expose them via preload contextBridge and check window.__VAR__ before import.meta.env.

title: fix(browser): reload page when pressing Enter on unchanged URL
summary: Electron webview loadURL() is a no-op when URL is unchanged — must call reload() explicitly for same-URL Enter-key navigation

title: fix(e2e): stabilize suite after WebContentsView migration
summary: WebContentsView migration lands 6 simultaneous bugs: each view has its own V8 context/Zustand store (broadcasts miss cached views), getAllAppWebContents iterated only active webContents, E2E getActiveAppWindow returned welcome appView due to children order, useSearchablePalette stale closure from non-memoized getItemId, default LRU=1 evicted onboarding, E2E ctx.window cached pre-switch. Audit every 'one renderer' assumption.

title: fix(persistence): capture and flush state before project view teardown
summary: WebContentsView eviction on project switch destroys webContents before beforeunload + debounced (500ms) IPC flush completes — browser/dev-preview panels (renderer-only state, no PTY anchor) vanish. Fix: capture outgoing snapshot synchronously in renderer, embed in switchProject IPC payload, main writes before swapping views.

title: fix(dock): restore dock visibility and click handlers
summary: setTimeout(250ms) click/dblclick arbitration via e.detail introduces visible delay and double-fires after dblclick handled. Separate handlers are mandatory. Also: registry-level Zustand functions bypass store-wrapper cleanup (moveTerminalToGrid does NOT clear activeDockTerminalId — only the store wrapper does). useMemo over Zustand slices must include all accessed fields in deps.

title: fix(e2e): resolve window staleness in nightly E2E core and online tests
summary: Playwright Electron: never cache electronApp.firstWindow() — WebContentsView project switches silently invalidate the reference, next interaction throws 'Target page/context/browser has been closed'. Use a getWindow() helper that resolves fresh at point of use.

title: feat(dev-preview): OAuth loopback flow for external auth redirects
summary: OAuth in Electron dev-preview requires RFC 8252 loopback server (not popup BrowserWindow). Google blocks Electron user-agent; popup violates RFC 8252 §8.12. Pattern: capture sessionStorage, spawn loopback server, use CDP to restore state.

title: fix(voice): handle Windows backslash paths in voice correction
summary: projectPath.split('/') in shared/ code fails on Windows — whole C:\Users\... path becomes one segment. Use split(/[/\\]/).filter(Boolean) since Node path module isn't available in renderer. Bug hit voiceCorrection.ts plus 4 renderer components.

title: fix(agent-state): prevent agent terminal getting stuck in working state
summary: ActivityMonitor CPU-high deferral loop had no upper bound — a background dev server kept agent terminals stuck in 'working' forever. Cap at MAX_CPU_HIGH_DURATION_MS (~5min). Also: emit pending state transition BEFORE checking disposed flag on teardown, and clear pattern buffers on timeout to prevent stale regex matches from blocking transitions.

title: fix(hydration): restore agent terminal panels on cold app restart
summary: PTY reconnect result 'not_found' means different things by context: cold start = PTY host not running yet (respawn), project switch = PTY genuinely gone (discard as phantom). Stale phantom-agent guard dropped all saved agent panels on every cold restart. Gate on _switchId !== undefined (only set during live switches).

title: fix(terminal): reserve WebGL for agent terminals and fix reflow race condition
summary: Sharing xterm WebGL context pool between standard and agent terminals causes transient blank frames when release/reacquire cycles leave standard terminals with no renderer. Reserve WebGL exclusively for agent panelKind in acquireWebGLLease; standard terminals use DOM unconditionally. Always check isDisposed at top of onContextLoss/onContextRestore — they can fire post-dispose().

title: fix(settings): project settings changes not persisting after dialog consolidation
summary: Multiple useEffect calls sharing a flag (projectIsInitialized) execute in declaration order on the same render — an init effect followed by a reset effect will suppress itself on dialog open. Consolidate into a single effect keyed on [projectId, isOpen] with conditional branches. Also: scope Zustand subscriptions to fields the form reads, not the whole slice.

title: fix(ui): recipe editor dialog overflow scrolling
summary: h-full does not establish a scrollable container in flex contexts. Use flex flex-col flex-1 min-h-0 — min-h-0 overrides the default min-height:auto that prevents flex children from shrinking.

title: fix(clone): prevent clone dialog from hanging and add cancellation support
summary: hardenedGit strips credential.helper/core.askpass/core.sshCommand — correct for background polling, but without GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 a user-initiated clone blocks indefinitely waiting for interactive prompts that can't reach an Electron context. Use separate gitForClone()/createAuthenticatedGit() for user-triggered ops (clone/push/pull) with AbortSignal + 5min hard timeout.

title: feat(git): support credentials for user-initiated git operations
summary: Split git into createHardenedGit (background polling, blanks core.askpass/credential.helper/core.sshCommand) and createAuthenticatedGit (user ops inherit keychain/gh auth/SSH agent); route by intent

title: fix(dock): prevent state corruption on docked terminal expand and project switch
summary: Five dock/grid terminal state corruption bugs: click/double-click race, no ownership check on xterm unmount, hydration overwriting dock location, and single vs grouped move path inconsistency. Use mountGeneration counter and ownership checks.

title: Windows blank terminal from fit/resize race on tab switch
summary: Windows blank xterm on tab switch: visibilitychange listener must be useLayoutEffect (not useEffect) to avoid missing render-to-effect gap; add sync missed-event recheck; do NOT gate ResizeObserver on document.visibilityState

title: E2E flakiness: fixed waits between spawns cause non-deterministic panel counts
summary: Playwright E2E: fixed T_SETTLE waits between terminal spawns go nondeterministic on Windows; clickToolbarButton without noWaitAfter:true double-dispatches from post-click network wait racing the handler

title: Windows registry PATH values contain unexpanded %VAR% references
summary: Windows registry REG_EXPAND_SZ PATH returns literal %USERPROFILE% tokens unexpanded; must call expandWindowsEnvVars before use. Node tool managers (npm AppData\Roaming, Volta, pnpm, fnm, nvm-windows) not in system PATH

title: Recipe merge priority was inverted: local shadowed in-repo instead of vice versa
summary: Recipe merge priority was inverted: project-local shadowed in-repo recipes instead of the reverse; in-repo (git-tracked) must always win on name collision

title: Worktree delete dialog: untracked files incorrectly triggered uncommitted-changes warning
summary: git worktree remove blocks only on tracked changes, not untracked files; WorkspaceService must split trackedChangeCount and untrackedFileCount so delete dialog doesn't false-warn on untracked-only worktrees

title: Phantom agent terminals restored on project open due to incomplete orphan filter
summary: Phantom agent panels on project open: stateHydration orphan filter only guarded default- prefixed terminals; exited agents (hasPty false/undefined, exit 0) slipped through both orphan filter and reconnect guard

title: Panel split ratios keyed by worktree caused stale ratios for different panel pairs
summary: Panel split ratios keyed by worktree ID caused stale ratios from a previous panel pair to apply to a different pair on the same worktree; key by the specific panel pair tuple instead

title: navigator.clipboard.write() with binary PNG data crashes renderer on Linux in Electron 41
summary: navigator.clipboard.write() with binary PNG data crashes Electron 41 renderer on Linux at the native level, bypassing JS error handling; always route image clipboard writes through IPC to main process using electron.clipboard.writeImage()

title: PTY data events need adaptive batching on the MessagePort path to avoid IPC overload
summary: PTY data events forwarded 1:1 to MessagePort caused IPC overload during bulk output; adaptive PortBatcher using setImmediate for idle and 16ms intervals for bulk significantly reduces message volume

title: Browser/DevPreview panels restore stale URLs that connect to nothing after session reload
summary: Browser/DevPreview restore stale persisted browserUrl to dead dev server; clear URL on mount when no dev server configured, detect -102 ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on initial load (isInitialLoad ref) to show inline message

title: Ubuntu AppImage blank terminal: 0x0 initial dimensions and stale resize cache suppression
summary: Ubuntu AppImage blank terminal: containers report 0x0 at mount so debounced fit skips; bypass debounce for first non-zero observation and never use stale lastWidth/lastHeight cache to suppress resizes without checking proposeDimensions()

title: Terminal opening in home dir due to worktree init race
summary: Terminal spawns in home dir due to renderer race: useActiveWorktreeSync emits before worktrees hydrate; fix adds isInitialized guard and async worktree path lookup in main process lifecycle.ts

title: tsc -b overwrites esbuild-bundled preload during typecheck
summary: tsc -b on preload tsconfig overwrites esbuild-bundled preload in dist-electron/, stripping bundled dependencies; fix: use tsc --noEmit -p for typecheck-only targets owned by esbuild

title: Git fsmonitor/untrackedCache causes cross-contamination of status across concurrent worktrees
summary: Concurrent worktree git status calls cross-contaminate counts via shared fsmonitor/untrackedCache daemon; fix: disable both in hardenedGit and stagger startup refresh

title: lastKnownProjectId fallback in TerminalRegistry caused phantom terminals after project switch
summary: lastKnownProjectId fallback in TerminalRegistry matched agent terminals to wrong project indefinitely after project switch — removed the fallback entirely

title: MessagePort ACK before xterm write made renderer watermarks ineffective
summary: MessagePort path ACKed data on receipt (before xterm write), making renderer watermarks ineffective at full wire speed; fix: defer ACK to terminal.write() callback matching IPC path

title: Unbounded coalesced batch in TerminalOutputIngestService caused multi-MB xterm.write() calls
summary: xterm terminal.write() blocks renderer main thread proportional to input size; unbounded Array.join() during backpressure drain bursts produced multi-MB single writes. Cap at 256 KB, defer remainder next tick.

title: Awaiting recipe loading during hydration blocked first paint by 50-160ms
summary: Awaiting recipeLoadPromise in stateHydration blocked isStateLoaded flip by 50-160ms on cold start; project-switch path was already fire-and-forget; remove await to match.

title: Incremental restore left dangling timeout handles per chunk on success
summary: setTimeout ID not captured in TerminalRestoreController race-against-callback pattern; 8 dead 5s timers per 256KB incremental restore accumulated across hibernation cycles.

title: Small restore path lacked restoreGeneration check — stale callback corrupted new terminal
summary: TerminalRestoreController small path only checked object identity, missing restoreGeneration counter guard; stale callback after unhibernation corrupted new terminal scroll offset. Incremental path already had the guard.

title: Background-window polling throttle using BrowserWindow focus events and powerMonitor battery
summary: All polling services ran at full rate when window was backgrounded; WindowFocusThrottle class added using BrowserWindow focus/minimize events and powerMonitor battery state to apply 5x multiplier

title: V8 compile cache not applied to UtilityProcess entry points — startup penalty on every launch
summary: v8.enableCompileCache() must run before any import/require to take effect; UtilityProcess entry points (pty-host, workspace-host) need thin bootstrap wrappers with dedicated userData subdir, not OS temp.

title: perf(event-inspector): batch IPC broadcasts with 50ms flush window
summary: EventInspector broadcast every event via individual IPC (hundreds/sec during agent bursts); per-subscriber 50ms flush buffer cuts IPC volume 90%+. Per-subscriber timers isolate slow consumers.

title: perf(terminal): pre-compute command queue count per terminal
summary: Zustand Array.filter selector for per-terminal command queue count was O(N*M) on every TerminalPane render; maintain countById Record at write-time (queue/process/clear) — selector becomes O(1) primitive, useShallow unneeded.

title: perf(workspace): add singleflight cache to getAllStatesAsync
summary: WorkspaceClient.getAllStatesAsync had 11 call sites with no dedup; concurrent callers each IPC'd separately. Singleflight Map<key,Promise> with 150ms TTL collapses bursts; evict on error; use Promise.allSettled for multi-host fan-out not sequential await.

title: perf(terminal): use replaceChildren() to clear hibernated terminal DOM
summary: while(el.firstChild) removeChild loop triggers N layout invalidations per child; replaceChildren() batches into single C++ ContainerNode::RemoveChildren call. Safe in Electron 41 (Chromium 146). Call terminal.dispose() before DOM clear.

title: refactor(platform): replace scattered navigator.platform with shared utility
summary: navigator.platform is undefined in preload/workers/SSR contexts — unguarded access throws. Centralize with typeof navigator guard and cached result; tests mocking navigator.platform need vi.resetModules() to clear module cache between cases.

title: fix(menu): replace role menu items with correct webContents targets
summary: After WebContentsView migration, all Electron menu `role` properties targeting webContents silently no-op. Edit roles need getFocusedWebContents(); app-specific actions need getAppWebContents(). Role system is hardwired to BrowserWindow.webContents.

title: feat(demo): upgrade capture pipeline to raw bitmap piping with text-crisp encoding
summary: toPNG() has 20-50ms/frame zlib overhead, capping capture at ~10-15 FPS. toBitmap() returns raw BGRA for direct ffmpeg stdin pipe at 25-35 FPS. Use libx264rgb 4:4:4 not yuv420p for text-crisp encoding of IDE content.

title: fix(worktree): replace session resume with terminal history injection for worktree transfer
summary: Agent session resume is directory-scoped and fails cross-worktree. Claude Code sessions live under ~/.claude/projects/{path-encoded}/ and cannot be resumed from a different path. Fix: capture xterm.js buffer, strip ANSI, inject as context in fresh session.

title: Module-level Zustand subscriptions in hooks leak and act on stale state after unmount
summary: Module-level Zustand store.subscribe() outside useEffect never unsubscribes; async callbacks fire after unmount touching stale state. All subscriptions and window listeners must live in useEffect with cleanup; async paths need a disposed flag.

title: N+1 IPC pattern in getBulkStats and silent isTrashed bug in pty-host
summary: getBulkStats looped getTerminalAsync per terminal: ~180 pty-host IPC round-trips every 10s. Replace with single getAllTerminalsAsync + in-memory group. Also: isTrashed read from serialized TerminalInfo (silently undefined) instead of pty-host registry map.

title: xterm scrollback multipliers have outsized memory impact at scale
summary: xterm scrollback multipliers compound badly at scale: 12 agents × 2500 lines × 160 cols × ~1.5KB/line = 50-60MB in scroll buffers alone. Always multiply limit × instances × bytes_per_unit before setting defaults.

title: Each cached WebContentsView is a full Chromium renderer process (~400–500 MB)
summary: Each cached WebContentsView is a full Chromium renderer process (400-500MB). Defaulting LRU cache to 2 means ~1GB renderer memory even for single-project users. Default conservatively to 1; document per-view cost near the constant.

title: selectedIndex prop causes O(n) re-render cascade in list components; nested TooltipProvider is expensive
summary: Passing selectedIndex numeric prop to list items re-renders all N on every keypress; items.indexOf in render is O(n^2). Use isSelected boolean + React.memo. TooltipProvider nested per-item (45-60 instances/keystroke) must hoist to wrap list once.

title: Hover prefetch spawned UtilityProcess workspace hosts with a 3-minute cleanup delay
summary: onMouseEnter prefetch spawned UtilityProcess workspace hosts per hovered project with 3-minute cleanup grace; scanning 5 items = 5 live background Node.js processes. Measure cold path before adding prefetch — UtilityProcess spawns are not cheap.

title: Fuse.js index rebuilt on every stats poll because sortedProjects returned a new array reference
summary: Fuse.js index rebuilt every 10s because sortedProjects selector returned a new array reference on each stats push; memo dep was the array not stable identity. Key memo on sorted-IDs string so index only rebuilds on project identity change, not stats.

title: transition-all forces Chromium to interpolate every computed CSS property on every frame
summary: transition-all forces Chromium to interpolate every computed CSS property on every frame; 62 instances replaced with specific transition properties (transition-colors, transition-[width], etc.); establish correct defaults in base components like button.tsx early

title: useMemo depending on worktreeMap invalidated progress list mid-execution, hiding completed items
summary: Progress dialog derived via useMemo from worktreeMap: completed items vanished mid-run because 'already exists' filter (correct pre-exec) became destructive during exec. Snapshot plan into useRef at exec start, render from snapshot.

title: CSS layout containment to isolate terminal panel style recalculations
summary: CSS `contain: layout style` on grid cell wrappers prevents style recalculations in one terminal panel from cascading across the whole grid. Don't use `paint`/`strict` if children need to overflow for tooltips.

title: Clamp oversized window to display before centering on restore
summary: Window restore after monitor removal: clampToDisplay must reduce width/height to work area, not just x/y. win.center() collapses oversized windows on macOS. Off-screen recovery must also trigger when oversized at origin.

title: Port-per-view worktree architecture rewrite
summary: Global Zustand worktree store couldn't be made safe for multi-window: 9 incremental scope-guard fixes failed because the singleton itself was the problem. Rewrite to MessagePort-per-view with per-view store factory gives structural isolation.

title: Reentrancy guard for async terminal spawn handlers
summary: Async spawn handler fired twice on rapid Enter: React events fire faster than IPC round-trip. Use useRef (synchronous) not useState (batched) for reentrancy guard. Also set type="button" to avoid form submit re-trigger.

title: Naive path.includes('..') check rejects valid nested-directory paths
summary: path.includes('..') is wrong for path traversal guards — it's a substring match that rejects valid paths like 'components/Button.tsx'. Use segment-by-segment check: split on /\ and reject only exact '..' segments.

title: fixPath() must be guarded by app.isPackaged; PATH must be refreshed before first CLI check
summary: fixPath() must be guarded by app.isPackaged — in dev it overwrites the inherited terminal PATH. CliAvailabilityService must call refreshPath() lazily before first check, not at module load time before PATH fixup runs.

title: React 19 Activity API is not applicable to multi-renderer Electron architectures
summary: React 19.2 Activity API preserves tree state across visibility transitions but only within a single renderer. Electron per-project WebContentsView = separate renderer processes, no shared tree. Vestigial key={activeProjectId} at App root was forcing full remounts.

title: Electron will-navigate handler silently blocked all cross-origin navigations
summary: Electron will-navigate handler blocked all cross-origin navigations, silently breaking OAuth/OIDC flows. Should only block non-HTTP/HTTPS schemes. Always emit an IPC event to renderer when blocking so the UI can surface the reason.

title: Per-project Electron sessions duplicate V8 bytecode and HTTP caches
summary: Per-project Electron session partitions duplicate V8 bytecode and HTTP caches for identical renderer bundles. Use a single shared persist:canopy-app session for all project WebContentsViews; keep separate partitions only for webviews that browse external URLs.

title: Webview partition not propagated in will-attach-webview; localStorage lost on tab eviction
summary: Two Electron webview bugs: (1) will-attach-webview must explicitly set webPreferences.partition or Electron falls back to ephemeral session; (2) must await session.flushStorageData() before destroying WebContents to avoid losing buffered localStorage writes.

title: Trigger V8 GC after deactivating cached WebContentsViews
summary: V8's lazy GC leaves 10-30MB of unmounted React tree on the heap after view deactivation. Trigger window.gc() via webContents.executeJavaScript() with 500ms delay after view goes off-screen to reclaim memory promptly.

title: Global disable-renderer-backgrounding flags override per-view throttling
summary: Global disable-renderer-backgrounding Chromium flag overrides setBackgroundThrottling(true) on individual WebContentsViews — the per-view call becomes a silent no-op. Remove global flags and rely on per-view throttling in ProjectViewManager.

title: Chromium feature flags for memory reclamation in Electron apps
summary: Chromium feature flags for Electron memory optimization: enable PartitionAllocMemoryReclaimer + IntensiveWakeUpThrottling, disable BackForwardCache. Note: V8FlushBytecodeOnContextDispose is an internal V8 flag and must NOT be passed via enable-features.

title: WebContentsView-per-project eliminates cross-project state contamination
summary: WebContentsView-per-project gives each project its own V8 heap, eliminating all store-reset and hydration-race logic. Switch time drops from 500-1500ms to <16ms. BrowserWindow.fromWebContents() fails for WebContentsView senders — requires a webContentsRegistry. Migration deleted 3,153 lines of guard/reset code.

title: Old workspace host leaks events after project switch — 8th recurrence
summary: 8th recurrence of cross-project contamination: old workspace host still alive during 5s grace emits events that pass generation-counter guards because new listeners are set up at the current generation. Root causes: double forceReinitialize call, routeHostEvent never checking projectScopeId, snapshot validation only covering the main worktree.

title: Fetch fork PR branches via pull/N/head:branch before worktree creation
summary: Fork PR branches aren't in the upstream remote's normal branch refs. Use git fetch origin pull/N/head:branchName — the same synthetic ref GitHub exposes for all PRs including forks, same as gh pr checkout. Fetch before worktree creation; remove all isForkPR gates.

title: Blue-green host swap pattern for workspace host transitions
summary: Blue-green swap pattern for workspace host replacement: spawn new, wait for ready signal, hand off, kill old. Generation counters handle rapid A→B→C switching. Track git child PIDs and kill on host exit to prevent .git/index.lock orphans.

title: Per-project workspace host processes — architectural solution to recurring contamination
summary: After 7 previous 'fixes' and 16 guard mechanisms, cross-project contamination was solved by spawning one utility process per project. Singleton processes with shared state cannot be made safe via application-level guards. WorkspaceClient becomes a process lifecycle manager; renderer IPC contract is unchanged.

title: PTY host multi-port routing: per-window MessagePort connections with project filtering
summary: PTY host singleton rendererPort replaced with Map<windowId, RendererConnection>. connect-port, set-active-project, and project-switch messages carry windowId. Per-window project tracking filters terminal output. Clean up on MessagePort 'close' event.

title: Multi-window: removing single-window guard and scoping per-window state
summary: Removing Electron's single-window guard requires: unique terminal ID prefixes per window to avoid PTY routing collisions, skipping global service re-init on subsequent windows, and changing second-instance handler from focus-existing to create-new.

title: IPC broadcast vs. targeted routing in multi-window Electron apps
summary: Multi-window Electron: webContents.send() loops broadcast IPC to all windows. For request-response, capture event.sender at handler entry. For push events, use BrowserWindow.fromWebContents() ownership; fallback broadcast on null.

title: Re-implement MessagePort backpressure with string-based acks after Buffer pool corruption
summary: Node.js Buffer.from() shares an 8KB slab pool — transferring bytes.buffer over MessagePort detaches the entire pool. Use strings (not binary) for PTY host transport; acks via string messages avoid all shared-memory issues. React StrictMode double-mount also breaks capacity-based backpressure.

title: hover:text-text-inverse renders invisible in dark themes — use text-canopy-text instead
summary: hover:text-text-inverse resolves to dark text in dark themes (Daintree), making hovered text invisible. 'Inverse' means inverted from normal text color, not high-contrast. Use text-canopy-text for readable hover text in any theme.

title: Adaptive resource profiles: hysteresis prevents oscillation when scaling system parameters
summary: Adaptive resource profiles (Performance/Balanced/Efficiency) driven by memory pressure, event loop lag, battery state, and worktree count. Central coordinator broadcasts profile IPC; each process applies locally. Hysteresis (30s down, 60s up) prevents oscillation.

title: Embedding iframes in Electron: youtube-nocookie.com + sandbox attributes for CSP compliance
summary: Embed YouTube iframes in Electron using youtube-nocookie.com + sandbox attributes for CSP compliance. Gate the feature by explicit panel type flag (isHelpPanel), not URL inspection. Strip ANSI codes from terminal output before URL matching. Cap embeds per panel at 3.

title: refactor(window): scope per-window services into WindowContext.services
summary: Multi-window Electron: storing per-window services as module-level singletons silently destroys other windows' services on close. Per-window services (PortalManager, EventBuffer, MessagePorts) must live in WindowContext.services; global services (PtyClient, WorkspaceClient) stay module-level and only dispose when the last window closes.

title: fix(browser): surface blocked cross-origin navigations with Open in Browser action
summary: Electron webview will-navigate block silently swallows OAuth (RFC 8252 forbids embedded OAuth; Google enforces). Emit WEBVIEW_NAVIGATION_BLOCKED IPC with canOpenExternal flag; only offer external for http/https, never javascript/data/file/about.

title: fix(dev-preview): auto-inject --turbopack for Next.js dev servers in webview
summary: Next.js webpack mode shows blank/unstyled pages in Electron webview because style-loader CSS injection fails silently. Fix: auto-inject --turbopack at both detection time (findDevServerCandidate) and runtime (DevPreviewSessionService). Works for all package managers, idempotent.

title: feat(pty-host): enable bidirectional MessagePort for terminal output
summary: Electron UtilityProcess cannot transfer SharedArrayBuffers — the entire SAB ring buffer architecture was dead code. Correct high-throughput path: MessagePort (CAN be transferred to UtilityProcess), with watermark-based PortQueue backpressure and PTY pause/resume coordination. PtyPauseCoordinator must support multiple concurrent pause reasons.

title: feat(diagnostics): rich diagnostics badge with popover
summary: Diagnostics trend: use 12-sample sliding-window linear regression (MB/min) over 60s, not delta-of-last-two which is noisy due to GC. Poll only while popover open (wire to onOpenChange). Absolute MB thresholds, not percentages.

title: feat(recipes): file export/import and in-repo recipe storage
summary: secureEnvVars must be stripped before writing recipes to tracked git paths (.canopy/recipes/). In-memory store retains full data; sanitizeRecipeForRepo() strips secrets from disk representation.

title: feat(memory): reduce memory via completed-agent hibernation and lower thresholds
summary: xterm.js instances hold scrollback + addons + canvas (tens of MB each). Completed agent terminals stay VISIBLE tier forever under idle-timer hibernation. Use push model: hibernateCompletedAgentTerminals() triggered by agent completion events.

title: feat(project-switcher): context menu pins, status dots, modifier hints, section split
summary: Modifier key state hooks must reset on window blur to prevent stuck keys. If user Cmd+Tabs away while holding a modifier, keyup never fires in the losing window.

title: fix(dock): route Escape to terminal/editor when it has focus in dock popover
summary: Radix UI DismissableLayer intercepts Escape in event capture phase before xterm.js sees it. Fix: use onEscapeKeyDown prop with focus-based guard to conditionally block popover dismissal when terminal has focus.

title: feat(panels): allow terminals and browsers without a project open
summary: node-pty cwd='' is NOT cwd=~ — empty string inherits process cwd unpredictably. When currentProject is null, fall back to os.homedir(). Keep ContentGrid mounted always; render WelcomeScreen as empty state inside it, not as replacement.

title: fix: preserve webview partition + flush storage before tab destruction
summary: Two independent webview storage bugs in Electron: partition not propagated through will-attach-webview handler, and storage flush race when destroying tabs before pending writes reach disk.

title: feat(sound): migrate sound playback from OS process spawning to Web Audio API
summary: Electron renderer Web Audio sound playback: pre-decode WAVs at startup. Must serve via custom app:// protocol — file:// blocked by CSP. AudioContext auto-suspends when idle.

title: feat(sounds): add sound dampening for rapid consecutive events
summary: Sound dampening: 150ms debounce on BASE name (variants bypass naive dedup), 70% exp volume decay over 2s, 3-voice priority pool. afplay --volume 0-1; paplay --volume 0-65536; Windows no-op.

title: feat(activity): use process CPU metrics to prevent premature idle transitions
summary: Agent idle detection: use process CPU (10% threshold) as tiebreaker vs output silence. macOS %cpu has 30-60s decay lag reading high after stop — WorkingSignalDebouncer absorbs it.

title: feat(confetti): use active theme colors for celebration particles
summary: Theme-aware runtime visuals: read CSS custom props via getComputedStyle(documentElement) at RENDER time, not module load. Fallback array required for tests where CSS isn't applied.

title: feat(terminal): detect and alert on terminal process memory leaks
summary: Terminal RSS leak detection: 10 consecutive polls (~25s) of NEAR-monotonic growth above 500MB — strict monotonic false-positives on spike-then-plateau indexing. Block auto-restart during active user input.

title: fix(worktree): use async rate limit queue for batch worktree creation
summary: IPC batch failed at EXACTLY 10: used checkRateLimit (sync throw) instead of waitForRateLimitSlot (async queue). Shared 'gitOps' category means unrelated git ops consume worktree quota.

title: Defer scrollback restore to after project switch completes
summary: Project-switch overlay blocked by eager scrollback IPC in stateHydration. Restore layout in critical path, defer scrollback via requestIdleCallback for active worktree; background worktrees lazy-restore.

title: Sound file paths broken in dev and packaged builds
summary: Electron ASAR: files spawned by EXTERNAL OS processes (afplay, paplay, PowerShell) cannot read inside app.asar — needs asarUnpack AND extraResources. Use app.isPackaged + process.resourcesPath in prod.

title: Open union type pattern preserves autocomplete while allowing plugin extensions
summary: TypeScript open union for plugin IDs: `BuiltInActionId | (string & {})` keeps autocomplete while accepting arbitrary strings. Plain `string` collapses union and loses suggestions.

title: Radix UI dropdown dismisses before Playwright click lands on Windows
summary: Radix dropdowns dismiss before Playwright click on Windows: synthesized click fires focus change mid-sequence, triggering onOpenChange. Fix: ensureWindowFocused() then hover() before click().

title: electron-updater requires allowDowngrade: true when switching from nightly to stable
summary: electron-updater refuses nightly→stable because version cmp treats it as downgrade regardless of feed URL. Set autoUpdater.allowDowngrade = channel !== 'stable' on init AND setChannel().

title: fix(workspace): fix git polling queue concurrency
summary: PQueue gotcha: pollQueue.clear() removes QUEUED but does NOT cancel in-flight work. Pass AbortController signal to queue.add() and abort in stop(). Add per-monitor dedup guard for rapid intervals.

title: feat(build): add better-sqlite3 ABI validation and smoke check
summary: better-sqlite3 on Electron Windows: prebuild-install SILENTLY wins against electron-rebuild, fetching Node ABI not Electron ABI → ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED. Add afterPack ABI check + Database(':memory:') smoke.

title: fix(terminal): apply resize synchronously for warm terminals during project switch
summary: xterm warm-reattach resize flash: setTargetSize() only resized inside attach() via rAF (needed cold for WebGL DOM init). Warm path must call terminal.resize() synchronously before first paint.

title: fix(browser): support IPv6 localhost and unsafe-eval in webview CSP
summary: Webview CSP for local dev: isLocalhostUrl must match [::1] (IPv6 loopback default on modern stacks). script-src needs 'unsafe-eval' — Vite HMR uses eval() for module updates.

title: fix(cli): add multi-strategy Linux binary discovery and PATH symlink
summary: Linux binary discovery: AppImage runs from /tmp/.mount_*/ — use $APPIMAGE env var, not command -v. .deb to /opt/Canopy/ with NO PATH symlink — add postinst creating /usr/bin/canopy-app symlink.

title: fix(pty): replace wmic with PowerShell Get-CimInstance for process start time on Windows
summary: wmic REMOVED from Windows 11 (deprecated 10 21H1). Use PowerShell Get-CimInstance Win32_Process — output ISO DateTime (wmic was YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.mmmmm±ZZZ), update parse regex. Silent catch→null hid it.

title: fix(menu): show 'About Canopy' instead of 'About canopy-app' on macOS
summary: Electron productName: without it, npm-safe 'name' leaks to macOS About menu. Adding productName ALSO changes app.getPath('userData') default — hardcode dev userData path first or lose dev state.

title: perf(git): enable built-in fsmonitor and untracked cache
summary: HARDENED_GIT_CONFIG: core.fsmonitor is NOT a security risk — it's an OS-level FS event daemon, not a hook point. Enable fsmonitor + core.untrackedCache to cut git status from 100s of ms to single-digit ms under worktree polling.

title: feat(theme): auto-switch theme when system appearance changes
summary: nativeTheme 'updated' fires before macOS appearance is stable — always debounce 300ms before reading shouldUseDarkColors

title: feat(store): atomic state swap for project switching
summary: Zustand project switch: two-phase reset+rehydrate causes visible empty-render flash (terminal registry = [] between phases). Do an atomic swap in one update; only run teardown on stores with listeners (PTY).

title: fix(cli): generate stable wrapper for AppImage CLI symlink
summary: AppImage FUSE mount path in process.resourcesPath is ephemeral — generate a wrapper script that uses $APPIMAGE env var at runtime instead of symlinking into the mount path

title: fix(git): deduplicate in-flight git status operations per worktree
summary: Polling + fs-watcher fire git status concurrently per worktree. Coalesce with in-flight Promise<Result> map keyed by path, clean up via .finally() (never leave failed promises stuck), expose forceRefresh bypass.

title: feat(worktree): add content-visibility auto to worktree cards
summary: content-visibility:auto breaks dnd-kit drag transforms — must be disabled (set to visible) while isDragging; use containIntrinsicSize with auto prefix to cache height

title: fix(browser): prevent dark-mode color-scheme inheritance into webview
summary: color-scheme is inherited into webview elements — Chromium built-in viewers (JSON viewer) read it and apply hardcoded colors for the wrong mode; reset with webview { color-scheme: normal }

title: fix(ui): constrain dropdown and context menu height to available viewport space
summary: Radix UI DropdownMenuContent/ContextMenuContent don't auto-constrain height. Add max-h-[var(--radix-*-content-available-height)] overflow-y-auto or menus overflow viewport on Linux CI (1200x763), breaking E2E clicks.

title: fix(pty-host): add PtyPauseCoordinator for coordinated PTY flow control
summary: node-pty pause/resume is not reference-counted. Multiple callers (ResourceGovernor, BackpressureManager, IpcQueueManager) fighting over the same PTY's pause state causes desync. Use a reference-counted PtyPauseCoordinator.

title: fix(terminal): add IME composition guard to xterm key handler
summary: xterm custom onKey handlers must guard Enter with event.domEvent.isComposing. Without it, voice-to-text and IME input causes Enter to be swallowed or arrive before composed text.

title: fix(shutdown): close SQLite connection explicitly during shutdown
summary: SQLite WAL needs explicit close to checkpoint cleanly; Electron app.exit(0) bypasses C++ destructor so -wal/-shm sidecars persist. Wire closeSharedDb() into shutdown sequence after saveAllProjectStates, before exit.

title: fix(editor): suppress unhandled rejection in macOS open fallback
summary: execa detached child process missing .catch(). Post-return rejections escape the surrounding try/catch. In Electron 37+ utility processes, unhandled rejections are fatal. All detached execa calls need .catch(()=>{}).

title: perf(v8): reduce heap limits and remove optimize-for-size
summary: --optimize-for-size degrades JIT throughput and is wrong for desktop IDEs. Correct heap limits: renderer 1536MB + semi-space 64MB, pty-host 512MB (off-heap I/O). Remove the flag wherever found.

title: feat(window): recover from renderer process crashes
summary: Electron render-process-gone recovery: 1st crash webContents.reload(); crash loop (2 in 60s) load static recovery.html with zero app-bundle deps (React may be broken). Wire unresponsive/responsive in prod only — HMR false positives in dev.

title: feat(disk-space): proactive disk space monitoring with user warnings
summary: Disk space monitor: use fs.statfs() for free bytes. Hysteresis thresholds (not single trigger) prevent notification spam. At critical levels, suppress non-essential writes (snapshots/backups) instead of escalating alerts. Propagate disk level to PTY host via IPC.

title: feat(crash-recovery): add crash loop protection with safe mode boot
summary: Electron crash-loop protection: disk-persisted counter + stability timer; safe mode skips state hydration; clean-shutdown sentinel prevents normal quits from incrementing counter; stop-relaunch threshold prevents auto-relaunch DoS.

title: feat(power): prevent system sleep during active agent work
summary: Electron powerSaveBlocker.start() returns an ID that must be tracked for release. Use counter (not boolean) for multi-agent tracking. 4h safety timeout prevents permanent blocker leak if agent state tracker gets stuck.

title: fix(fs): add atomic write utilities and migrate important file writes
summary: Atomic write pattern for Node/Electron: write .tmp, fsync, close, rename. fsync() is REQUIRED before rename on Linux—otherwise crash after rename but before cache flush loses data. Consolidate into shared util.

title: feat(persistence): SQLite backup, WAL checkpoint, and corruption recovery
summary: better-sqlite3 .backup() is the safe hot-backup API. WAL checkpoint must run before closeSharedDb(). Corruption recovery on startup: detect with PRAGMA quick_check, quarantine (rename to .corrupted-TIMESTAMP), restore from backup, else fresh init. Preserve corrupted files for forensics.

title: feat(lifecycle): add hard timeout to graceful shutdown cleanup chain
summary: Electron shutdown: wrap cleanup chain in Promise.race with hard timeout (~10s). Use app.exit(1) NOT app.quit() on timeout (quit re-triggers lifecycle). Track currentPhase variable for diagnostics. Clear timeout on normal completion.

title: fix(terminal): prevent agent terminal flash on focus
summary: When reparenting xterm.js elements between DOM containers, set opacity:0 before appendChild and restore opacity:1 inside the rAF callback after terminal.refresh(). Add a safety timeout to restore visibility if rAF stalls. Reset opacity on early-return paths.

title: fix(main): move logger init after single-instance lock
summary: initializeLogger() called clearSessionLogs() before app.requestSingleInstanceLock(), so a second instance wiped the running instance's logs. Any initialization with side effects on shared resources must come after the single-instance lock check.

title: fix(env): refresh PATH before recheck to detect newly installed tools
summary: Electron on macOS inherits launchd minimal PATH, not interactive shell PATH. To detect Homebrew/nvm/fnm tools, spawn login shell ($SHELL --login -c 'echo $PATH') per recheck. Cache with short TTL to batch recheck spawns.

title: fix(scripts): strip ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE from spawned Electron environments
summary: ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 inherited from VS Code terminals causes Electron to run as plain Node.js, making require('electron') return an empty object. Every script spawning Electron must explicitly delete this env var before spawning.

title: fix(agent-state): increase working recovery debounce to prevent false transitions on layout shifts
summary: 300ms workingRecoveryDelayMs was within the duration of layout shift transients (worktree switches, panel resizes), causing false waiting→working agent state transitions. Increased to 1500ms — real LLM output is sustained for seconds, so longer debounce has no impact on legitimate transitions.

title: fix(build): use node-pty prebuilds on Windows instead of source compilation
summary: electron-rebuild -f forces source compilation and bypasses prebuilts. On Windows this requires Spectre-mitigated MSVC libs (not in default C++ workload), breaking npm install. Switch to @electron/rebuild without -f to use prebuilts; add beforeBuild hook to copy prebuilts for packaging.

title: fix(pty): persist agent launch flags through crash recovery
summary: Crash recovery: agent launch flags (--dangerously-skip-permissions) must live in backend PTY state, not renderer state. Orphaned PTY rebuild reads TerminalPublicState; renderer-only flags are lost on crash/wipe.

title: fix(git): prevent 'external diff died' by removing broken diff.external= override
summary: Setting diff.external= (empty string) in git -c overrides does NOT disable external diff — git tries to execute the empty string as a program. Use --no-ext-diff flag on all git diff calls instead. Empty-string -c overrides for executable-valued git config keys are broken.

title: fix(review-hub): prevent commit textarea focus loss during re-renders
summary: React focus loss: textarea state inside polling-re-rendered parent loses focus mid-keystroke (git status poll unmounts subtree). Fix: lift state to parent as controlled prop, useRef in onChange to decouple from render, memoize input section.

title: feat(terminal): dispose xterm.js instances for background-tier terminals
summary: xterm.js Terminal instances (2-15 MB each) were never disposed for background terminals, only addons were released. After 30s in BACKGROUND tier, fully dispose the Terminal instance while PTY stays alive. Wake by recreating Terminal from PtyHost-serialized state via TerminalWakeManager.

title: feat(portal): LRU eviction for background WebContentsView tabs
summary: Portal tabs as WebContentsViews each spawn a Chromium process. LRU eviction keeps only 3 live views (active + 2 MRU). Eviction via webContents.close(); URL preserved for seamless recreation. Expose evicted tab IDs via IPC so renderer can show loading state on re-visit.

title: fix(editor): use platform-aware path methods for cross-platform test compatibility
summary: path.join() always uses host OS separator regardless of mocked process.platform. Tests mocking platform='darwin' on Windows get backslash paths and fail. Use path.posix.join() for macOS paths, path.win32.join() for Windows paths, selecting based on target platform.

title: feat(webview): lazy-load webviews for browser and dev-preview panels
summary: Lazy webview creation via IntersectionObserver + useHasBeenVisible (one-way first-visibility gate). Composable with useWebviewEviction—lazy creation and memory-pressure destruction are orthogonal. Saves Chromium processes for unvisited panels.

title: fix(terminal): hide shell init noise in agent terminals
summary: Shell init output visible before agent takes over terminal. Inject \x1b[3J\x1b[H\x1b[2J (clear scrollback + visible screen) immediately before agent command, after sentinel detection. \x1b[2J alone is insufficient — it leaves scrollback accessible via scroll-up.

title: fix(pty-host): remove manual global.gc() calls from PtyHost
summary: global.gc() in PtyHost UtilityProcess causes 200-400ms terminal freeze (all I/O blocked during GC) and increases heap fragmentation. V8's incremental GC handles freed objects automatically. The ResourceGovernor's terminal-pause mechanism already reduces allocation pressure sufficiently.

title: fix(pty): preserve user locale instead of hardcoding en_US.UTF-8
summary: fix(pty): preserve user locale instead of hardcoding en_US.UTF-8

title: fix(updater): add Linux guard for non-AppImage builds
summary: electron-updater on Linux throws 'APPIMAGE env is not defined' every check interval on .deb installs. Guard: skip initialize() if !process.env.APPIMAGE on Linux. Mirror of Windows PORTABLE_EXECUTABLE_FILE guard. Also applies to snap/Flatpak.

title: feat(pty-host): read PTY limit on Linux via /proc/sys/kernel/pty/max
summary: Linux PTY max: parseInt(fs.readFileSync('/proc/sys/kernel/pty/max')) — default 4096. macOS: sysctl kern.maxptys. Windows: N/A. FdMonitor needs all three for PTY exhaustion diagnostics in multi-agent workflows.

title: feat(terminal): derive hybrid input bar colors from xterm theme
summary: CodeMirror themes are build-time config, not CSS vars—xterm ITheme JS objects don't become CSS variables. Derive CM theme from ITheme via buildInputBarTheme(theme), store in Compartment, hot-swap via compartment.reconfigure() on scheme change.

title: fix(terminal): wait for shell init before injecting agent command
summary: fix(terminal): wait for shell init before injecting agent command

title: feat(project-switcher): batch IPC calls to prevent memory overload freeze
summary: Project switcher: N individual project:get-stats IPC calls froze renderer (heap spike) for 10+ projects. Batch via single getBulkStats endpoint + 30s TTL renderer cache. Threshold ~5 items.

title: feat(terminal): suppress resize events during sidebar toggle
summary: feat(terminal): suppress resize events during sidebar toggle

title: fix(dev-preview): detect URL on first spawn without restart cycle
summary: Dev-preview URL missed on first spawn: PTY data listener wired after IPC mirror pipe, Vite emits URL in ~300ms gap, triggering 10s STALE_START_RECOVERY restart. Fix: poll getRecentLines() every 500ms for 5s after spawn.

title: fix(terminal): prevent duplicate terminal tabs on project switch
summary: Duplicate terminal tabs on project switch: hook re-registered project-switched listener on each invocation (not once on mount) + event emitted from 3 sites (appLifecycle, menu, windowServices). Single canonical emit + mount-once listener.

title: fix(grid): restore layout correctly when exiting maximize mode
summary: Grid maximize restore dep cycle: effects depended on preMaximizeLayout they also cleared, interrupting restore. Use useRef mirror for read-without-react. Also use repeat(N, minmax(0,1fr)) + min-w-0 so grid children can shrink below content.

title: fix(bulk-command): prevent infinite re-render loop in BulkCommandPalette
summary: fix(bulk-command): prevent infinite re-render loop in BulkCommandPalette

title: fix(portal): catch unhandled promise rejection in PortalManager.navigate()
summary: Electron 37+ crashes utility processes on unhandled promise rejections. webContents.loadURL() rejects on DNS/network/aborted navigation/redirect errors. Every loadURL() call needs .catch() handler.

title: fix(agent): make error events no-op to eliminate false failed state
summary: AgentStateMachine: unconditional error→failed transition false-positives on noisy stderr. Make error events no-op; reserve failed for SIGSEGV/SIGABRT, forensic crashes, explicit agent:killed. Treat exit codes 0/1/137 as routine.

title: fix(worktree): scope group-hover with named groups to fix file text color
summary: Tailwind v4: plain `group` is shared across ancestors — nested group/group-hover at card+row levels both fire on card hover. Use named groups (group/card, group-hover/card:) to scope hover independently at each level.

title: fix(agent): reduce false-positive failed state detection and improve recovery
summary: terminalForensics.isRoutineExit() had inverted boolean logic — returned true for crashes, false for SIGINT(130)/SIGTERM(143)/SIGHUP(129). Agents showed failed after Ctrl+C. State machines also need recovery paths out of failed.

title: fix(grid): restore panel order after hydration
summary: Panel hydration creates new IDs; saved ordering array with old IDs falls back to default (lost layout). Remap via label matching: snapshot savedOrder pre-hydration, then restoreTerminalOrder(savedOrder, newPanels). Apply in app and project-switch rehydration.

title: fix(editor): add macOS .app bundle detection for external editors
summary: macOS: EditorService.findEditorBinary() missed .app bundles (users configure /Applications/IntelliJ IDEA.app, not CLI on PATH). Resolve via <bundle>/Contents/MacOS/<appname>. Add findInApplicationDirs() scanning /Applications + ~/Applications before PATH.

title: test(e2e): IPC listener and handler cleanup verification
summary: IPC listener/handler leak detection: expose ipcDiagnostics.getHandlerCount()/getListenerCount() through preload bridge. E2E pattern: baseline count → N open/close cycles → assert delta within threshold. Preload bridge is correct surface for main-process state.

title: feat(e2e): add nightly memory leak detection tests
summary: Memory leak thresholds: <20MB heap growth after 20 terminal cycles, <10MB external memory (node-pty native) after 10 cycles, error store bounded at 50 after 100 injections. Need process.memoryUsage() via main-process IPC (window.performance.memory is renderer-only).

title: test(e2e): add race condition tests for concurrent IPC operations
summary: UI debouncing serializes user operations and hides IPC race conditions. E2E: use page.evaluate() + Promise.all() to invoke IPC truly concurrently. Assert idempotency on same-ID spawns, queue overflow (51+) returns errors not hang, assert final state not ordering.

title: test(e2e): add error buffering flush E2E tests
summary: Testing 'buffer until renderer ready' paths: electronApp.evaluate() races window ready on fast machines. Dev-only __TEST_FAULT_INJECT global in errorHandlers.ts pushes directly into pendingQueue sync, bypassing canSendToRenderer() check.

title: fix(bulk-create): prevent double-execution and stale tracking ref
summary: Dialog double-execution: async handler dispatched START then awaited; button only hid on next render, allowing double-click. Parallel batchTrackingRef (outside reducer) not cleared on RESET, causing stale 'ghost' re-run with 0-of-N display. Guard with isExecutingRef + clear all parallel refs in handleClose/handleDone.

title: test(e2e): add terminal scrollback integrity under load test
summary: xterm.js scrollback tests must read scrollbackLimit via debug API (not hardcode 1000) and allow ±100 tolerance for shell prompt overhead. Assert post-flood interactivity — xterm can enter degraded state after 5000+ line bursts.

title: refactor(portal): rename Sidecar to Portal across entire codebase
summary: Cross-cutting renames (action IDs, IPC channels, types, stores, components) must be atomic because the cross-layer dependency graph won't compile at intermediate states. Zustand persistence key changes silently orphan old data — decide migrate vs. accept loss. Electron partition renames orphan cookies/auth tokens — document for users.

title: test(e2e): add panel drag-and-drop reordering tests
summary: @dnd-kit drag activation requires stepped mouse moves in CI/Xvfb. Use smoothDrag() helper with 20 incremental steps rather than Playwright's built-in dragTo().

title: fix(terminal): prevent agent terminal viewport from jumping to top
summary: xterm.js onScroll fires during programmatic buffer writes, not just user scroll. Use an _isWriting guard flag to prevent write-triggered scroll events from incorrectly setting isUserScrolledBack.

title: fix(workspace): detect main worktree by list position to prevent delete error
summary: isBareRepository heuristic fails for non-bare repos. Use git worktree list and check if path matches the FIRST entry — that is how Git defines the main worktree.

title: feat(activity-monitor): add prompt lexeme fallback heuristic for generic prompt detection
summary: Pattern-based detection misses novel/unknown prompt formats. Added three-tier hierarchy: fast pattern match (500ms) -> stall+lexeme heuristic at 3s (output quiet + last line has ?, :, y/n, no trailing newline) -> idle timeout at 6s. Matches pexpect/SWE-agent canonical approach.

title: feat(pty): add universal approval prompt hint patterns for all agents
summary: All 5 agents share [y/N], Approve, Allow, Deny vocabulary for tool approval prompts but none were in detection patterns. Agents stayed in 'working' for 6s on every approval prompt. UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_HINT_PATTERNS fallback layer added covering shared vocabulary.

title: fix(detection): correct Cursor and OpenCode agent detection patterns
summary: Cursor completion verbs incomplete (3 of 8 matched), whitespace regex too strict; OpenCode braille spinner chars copied from Gemini—entirely wrong Unicode code points; interrupt hint text differed. All caused agents to stay stuck in 'working' until 6s idle timeout.

title: feat(agent-detection): update Claude agent detection patterns for current CLI output
summary: Claude spinner char class missing middle dot (·, first animation frame) and 3 other chars actually used; hardcoded verb list broke users with custom spinnerVerbs (v2.1.23+); completion pattern matched old cost/token format instead of new 'Worked for Ns.' format.

title: feat(layout): respect grid capacity on undo and sync fit timing
summary: applySnapshot() bypassed getMaxGridCapacity(), restoring more panels than window supports; batch-fit timeout 150ms but CSS grid transition is 200ms, causing xterm to measure mid-animation fractional pixels. Fixed by clamping on restore and sharing GRID_TRANSITION_MS constant.

title: perf(terminal): simplify offscreen container CSS and use checkVisibility API
summary: Offscreen containers combined content-visibility:hidden + left:-20000px + pointer-events:none redundantly; -20000px forced wasted layout computation. ResizeController used rect.left < -10000 heuristic instead of checkVisibility() API.

title: feat(hibernation): protect active agents and git operations from hibernation
summary: Hibernation must scan for git sentinel files across ALL linked worktrees: index.lock, MERGE_HEAD, REBASE_HEAD, CHERRY_PICK_HEAD, rebase-merge/, rebase-apply/. Ignore stale locks older than inactivity threshold. Audit all hibernation entry points for ACTIVE_AGENT_STATES guard — scheduled and memory-pressure paths diverged.

title: feat(ui): improve DnD measurement stability and dock scroll robustness
summary: Three issues: (1) dnd-kit default bounding-box caching produces stale collision detection when CSS grid transitions run mid-drag — fix with MeasuringStrategy.Always + frequency:150; (2) missing overscroll-behavior-x:none on dock scroll container triggers macOS back-navigation gesture; (3) post-wake handlePostWake used pre-hibernation cached dimensions instead of calling fit(), and only fired for alt-screen terminals.

title: fix(pty-host): release SharedArrayBuffer references in cleanup
summary: PTY host cleanup() disposed managers but didn't nullify module-level SAB references (visualBuffers, visualSignalView, analysisBuffer). SABs are fixed non-movable V8 allocations — any lingering reference prevents GC. Existing null-guards made nullifying safe. Pattern: dispose then assign to empty/null, matching what ptyPool already did.

title: feat(ui): add global Escape key LIFO stack for layered UI dismissal
summary: Multiple UI components had independent window.addEventListener('keydown') Escape handlers with no coordination. Pressing Escape could close multiple layers simultaneously. Solution: LIFO stack with pushHandler/popHandler, single global dispatcher at app root, useEscapeStack hook for components. Radix onEscapeKeyDown must be replaced; chord-clearing in useGlobalKeybindings stays at capture phase for priority.

title: fix(terminal): restore clickable URLs by re-adding WebLinksAddon
summary: PR #3659 removed @xterm/addon-web-links as 'redundant' after adding backend UrlStyler. The backend UrlStyler and WebLinksAddon serve complementary roles — backend catches URLs pre-ANSI-processing, frontend addon is the safety net for chunk-split or ANSI-surrounded URLs. In xterm 6.0, OSC 8 native support only handles pre-formatted sequences; plain-text URL detection still requires the addon.

title: feat(terminal): add 'new output below' scroll indicator
summary: Overlays on xterm.js must use pointer-events:none on the container with pointer-events:auto only on the specific button. Standard overlay intercepts mouse events from xterm (selection, right-click, middle-click paste). Scroll state detection via xterm's onScroll + onData events, not DOM measurement. Use data-testid for E2E targeting.

title: fix(github): reduce bulk worktree queue concurrency and add throttling
summary: Bulk worktree creation with concurrency:4 and no inter-op delay caused .git/ lock contention for large batches (10+ items). Built-in retry logic perpetuated pressure by immediately queuing more operations. Fix: concurrency:2, PQueue intervalCap:1 + interval:300ms + strict:true for minimum spacing. Git locking is not designed for high concurrency from the same repo.

title: feat(memory): reclaim renderer memory on window minimize and memory pressure
summary: Electron --disable-renderer-backgrounding (needed for terminal throughput) disables Chromium's Blink cache reclamation. webFrame.clearCache() (preload-only API) is the ONLY way to return Blink memory to OS; global.gc() frees V8 JS but V8 keeps heap pages so RSS stays flat. Wire via IPC with 5s minimize debounce.

title: Expose GC in PTY host utility process
summary: Electron utility processes need --expose-gc in execArgv at spawn time or global.gc is undefined and silently no-ops. PTY host ResourceGovernor.engageThrottle() did nothing for months. global.gc() is sync/blocks event loop — only call post-hibernation or post-bulk-kill, never during streaming.

title: Add --optimize-for-size V8 flag to main process
summary: --optimize-for-size V8 flag is a memory win for I/O-bound Electron main process (apply via app.commandLine.appendSwitch('js-flags', ...)). Do NOT apply to PTY host utility process — real-time stream parsing needs JIT speed; the flag trades throughput for compact code.

title: Route PTY output over direct MessagePort
summary: Asymmetric MessagePort smell: renderer→PTY used direct port for input but PTY→renderer output still relayed through main process (4-hop). High-throughput output CPU-bottlenecked main. When routing output directly, IPC onData must check messagePortConnected flag to avoid duplicate delivery.

title: Transparent envelope for IPC error serialization
summary: Electron structured clone strips ALL non-standard Error properties across IPC — thrown Error with code:'FILE_LOCKED' arrives as generic Error in renderer. Wrap IPC responses in {ok, data|error} envelope and unwrap in preload _unwrappingInvoke to preserve custom error fields across process boundary.

title: Persist trashed terminal PIDs for crash-safe cleanup
summary: macOS has ~511 system-wide PTY file descriptors; orphaned PTYs from crashes during 20s trash grace window exhaust them. setTimeout-only tracking loses state on crash. Persist pending-kill PIDs to disk + validate process command name before SIGKILL on startup (PID reuse safety).

title: Stabilize activity object reference for React.memo
summary: React.memo defeated when buildPanelProps constructs inline {headline,status,type} object literal — Object.is always fails on new refs. Per-terminal activityCache Map keyed by constituent values fixes it. Upstream store early-exit guards don't help if prop assembly runs on any field change.

title: refactor(security): remove bypassCSP from canopy-file protocol
summary: Electron protocol.registerSchemesAsPrivileged bypassCSP:true is nuclear — disables ALL CSP for scheme, not just images. Correct fix: add canopy-file: to img-src in both DEV_CSP and PROD_CSP strings in vite.config.ts. Keep secure/supportFetchAPI privileges.

title: feat(terminal): wrap terminal writes with DEC Mode 2026 synchronized output
summary: xterm.js 6.0 supports DEC Mode 2026 (\x1b[?2026h / \x1b[?2026l) for Synchronized Output — wrap every terminal.write() to eliminate tearing on high-throughput agent output. Nested sequences are handled correctly. Overhead: 6 bytes/write ≈ 4.5KB/s at 750 writes/sec.

title: feat(terminal): dispose FileLinksAddon and ImageAddon for background-tier terminals
summary: xterm.js FileLinksAddon runs FILE_PATH_REGEX on every visible line during render — costly across 30-40 background agent terminals. Tier-manage dispose/recreate on BACKGROUND→VISIBLE transition. No public unloadAddon(); use addon.dispose() or IDisposable from registerLinkProvider().

title: feat(network): add fetch timeouts to GitHub API and Git operations
summary: simple-git constructor accepts timeout:{block:30_000}; Octokit/GraphQL needs explicit AbortController or AbortSignal.timeout(15_000). Without these, a slow GitHub API or git fetch hangs the whole feature indefinitely. Route timeout errors through parseGitHubError to serve stale cache instead of erroring.

title: fix(pty): kill orphaned PTY when TerminalProcess constructor throws
summary: node-pty PTY spawned in TerminalProcess constructor before fallible setup (agent config, headless terminal, handlers); throw leaves PTY orphaned because registry.add() runs AFTER constructor. Extract spawnPtyProcess() as standalone, try/catch, kill rawPty on failure. Separate acquisition from initialization.

title: feat(security): harden simple-git against malicious repo config RCE
summary: simple-git inherits malicious .git/config — core.fsmonitor runs arbitrary command on every git status/diff (CVE-2022-25912). Fix: createHardenedGit() passing -c core.fsmonitor=false, core.pager=cat, protocol.ext.allow=never + enforce path.isAbsolute(cwd) in validateCwd for renderer-supplied paths.

title: feat(pty): kill entire process tree on terminal close and app quit
summary: node-pty ptyProcess.kill() only SIGHUPs session leader; interactive shells setpgid() foreground commands (vite, next dev) into separate groups so even process.kill(-pid) misses them. Causes EADDRINUSE + macOS PTY exhaustion. Recursively pgrep -P, SIGTERM, escalate SIGKILL after 500ms. Windows: taskkill /T /F.

title: feat(csp): add form-action directive to webview CSP policy
summary: CSP form-action directive is NOT covered by default-src fallback and script-src doesn't restrict form submits — <form action=attacker.com> bypasses script-src entirely for HTML-injection exfiltration. Must list form-action explicitly in every webview CSP string.

title: perf(worktreeDataStore): preserve Map identity in mergeFetchedWorktrees
summary: Zustand Map stores: preserving individual entry refs isn't enough — returning new Map(...) container on every poll triggers all 12+ subscribers (default referential equality, no useShallow). Add size + per-entry identity check; return existingMap when all refs match to break the re-render cascade.

title: fix(activity-monitor): add disposed guard to prevent debounce timer leak
summary: Recursive setTimeout chains need a disposed flag check as FIRST line of callback — clearTimeout in dispose() only handles pre-fire timers, not callbacks already executing. Without it, PTY host crash leaves ActivityMonitor polling a dead terminal forever.

title: fix(security): restrict webview guest navigation to localhost URLs
summary: Electron webview guest navigation bypass: will-navigate on main window does NOT propagate to webview guest WebContents; must attach will-navigate in web-contents-created. setWindowOpenHandler only blocks window.open, not in-page nav.

title: feat(palette): replace manual debounce with useDeferredValue in all palettes
summary: React 19 useDeferredValue replaces manual setTimeout debounce in search palettes; adapts to render load automatically. Tests using jest.advanceTimersByTime must switch to act()/waitFor(). Centralize in shared hook.

title: feat(webview): validate webContentsId ownership in CDP handlers
summary: Electron CDP/debugger IPC handlers must validate webContentsId ownership via registry (e.g. WebviewDialogService.getPanelId) before attach; renderer can pass arbitrary IDs. Also: register-panel must fire BEFORE start-console-capture to avoid race.

title: fix(terminal): block OSC 52 clipboard hijacking from terminal output
summary: xterm.js 6.0 allowProposedApi:true enables OSC 52 clipboard writes - pastejacking vector via agent output. Block with terminal.parser.registerOscHandler(52, () => true) on every instance including prewarm. OSC 11 already overridden.

title: fix(hibernation): skip projects with falsy lastOpened instead of treating as epoch
summary: Never use `|| 0` fallback for timestamps in duration math — epoch 0 = 55 years ago = maximal staleness. Skip falsy timestamps instead. SQLite NOT NULL doesn't retroactively enforce on rows added pre-constraint via ALTER TABLE.

title: feat(terminal): use xterm.js onWriteParsed for adaptive output backpressure
summary: xterm.js 6.0 onWriteParsed event is the correct backpressure signal for output ingestion — fires once/frame when parser caught up. Replaces 8ms/25ms fixed flush timers. High/low watermarks 128KB/32KB for upstream pause/resume.

title: feat(pty-host): monitor PTY file descriptor usage to detect node-pty leaks
summary: feat(pty-host): monitor PTY file descriptor usage to detect node-pty leaks

title: perf(palette): wrap ActionPaletteItem and QuickSwitcherItem in React.memo
summary: React.memo on list items only works if ALL props stable — must also wrap parent callbacks in useCallback or memo misses. Two-step: memo child + useCallback parent. Profiling shows memo never hits if only one step done.

title: perf(selectors): optimize Zustand selectors to avoid unnecessary re-renders
summary: perf(selectors): optimize Zustand selectors to avoid unnecessary re-renders

title: feat(security): harden webview CSP and audit permission handlers
summary: Vite 6+ HMR no longer needs 'unsafe-eval' in CSP - remove it. Electron setPermissionRequestHandler/CheckHandler must have explicit default-deny for unknown permission types; otherwise new Electron versions add implicitly-allowed perms.

title: refactor(terminal): remove redundant WebLinksAddon
summary: xterm.js WebLinksAddon runs regex on every rendered line - expensive with many terminals. Backend OSC 8 hyperlinks (UrlStyler) replace it entirely; remove addon. Caveat: UrlStyler skips text with ANSI escapes to avoid breaking colors.

title: feat(renderer): add global unhandled promise rejection handler
summary: Electron renderer needs explicit window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection'|'error') — unlike main/utility processes (Electron 37+ crashes on unhandled rejection), renderer silently swallows them. Filter AbortError, re-entry protect, cleanup on HMR.

title: refactor(store): eliminate active tab state duplication between focus and registry slices
summary: refactor(store): eliminate active tab state duplication between focus and registry slices

title: fix(activity-monitor): add maximum working silence timeout for hung agents
summary: ActivityMonitor guard checking subprocess liveness needs absolute max-duration from lastDataTimestamp (WORKING_SILENCE_TIMEOUT_MS=3min). Alive process can be stalled. Also: onProcessExit hook forces idle on PTY EOF immediately.

title: feat(gpu): add GPU crash fallback to disable hardware acceleration
summary: feat(gpu): add GPU crash fallback to disable hardware acceleration

title: feat(perf): always-on event loop lag and long task monitoring
summary: Always-on perf monitoring needs startup suppression (5s) + rate limiting (1/10s) to avoid log flooding. Main process uses setInterval lag detector; renderer uses PerformanceObserver 'longtask' (100ms threshold always-on vs lower in capture mode).

title: fix(activity): stabilize agent state transitions to prevent working/waiting jitter
summary: fix(activity): stabilize agent state transitions to prevent working/waiting jitter

title: feat(skeleton): add startup skeleton UI shell to eliminate blank window
summary: Inline skeleton UI in index.html (CSS vars for theme, no JS) eliminates Electron blank-window flash before React mounts. Remove via requestIdleCallback with 3s safety timeout fallback; make removal idempotent. Mirror app layout to avoid CLS.

title: feat(watcher): replace timer-based git status with event-driven file watching
summary: Git file watcher must watch working directory (not just .git/index + refs/heads) — .git/index only updates on stage, not plain file saves. Add MAX_WAIT_MS=5s debounce ceiling, exponential retry for macOS fd exhaustion, keep timer as fallback.

title: fix(store): prevent input store state leaks across project switches
summary: resetAllStoresForProjectSwitch and per-terminal cleanup are separate flows - audit both when adding store. Mixing bare terminalId keys with composite projectId:terminalId in same store leaks history across projects; standardize on composite.

title: perf(renderer): enable V8 bytecode caching for faster startup
summary: perf(renderer): enable V8 bytecode caching for faster startup

title: fix(worktree): suppress WORKTREE_ACTIVATED echo for renderer-initiated activations
summary: Bidirectional IPC sync: unconditional echo-back from backend creates race where stale WORKTREE_ACTIVATED arrives after newer selection. Tag initiator (source:'renderer') and suppress echo to same side; renderer uses invoke return value instead.

title: fix(scheduler): replace requestIdleCallback with scheduler.postTask
summary: fix(scheduler): replace requestIdleCallback with scheduler.postTask

title: fix(drag): prevent iframe panels from stealing pointer events during drag
summary: fix(drag): prevent iframe panels from stealing pointer events during drag

title: fix(activity-monitor): add hysteresis to working/idle state transitions
summary: fix(activity-monitor): add hysteresis to working/idle state transitions

title: fix(terminal): two-phase directing state timeout for slow prompt composition
summary: fix(terminal): two-phase directing state timeout for slow prompt composition

title: fix(activity-monitor): prevent false idle during large non-polling output bursts
summary: fix(activity-monitor): prevent false idle during large non-polling output bursts

title: fix(terminal): immediately transition to working on Enter press
summary: fix(terminal): immediately transition to working on Enter press

title: fix(terminal): stop directing indicator firing on navigation and control keys
summary: fix(terminal): stop directing indicator firing on navigation and control keys

summary: Unblock alt screen and exclude CI env for Bubble Tea TUI agents

summary: Compute delta-based CPU% on Windows and fix activity fallback
